title: Fagara macrophylla
taxonomy: Fagara macrophylla is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Fagara macrophylla belongs to the genus Fagara, which is a genus of plants that are primarily found in Africa. The genus Fagara is often included within the broader genus Zanthoxylum, with the two being considered to be synonymous in some classifications. Further classification may also recognize Fagara macrophylla at the species level within the family Rutaceae.

intro: Fagara macrophylla, also known as the Chinese prickly ash, is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, characterized by its unique prickly branches and aromatic leaves. The health benefits of Fagara macrophylla include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include coumarins, limonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Fagara macrophylla is commonly prepared into teas, decoctions, and extracts, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to make patent medicines and tonics.
